cards.title: DiScoFormer: One Transformer Estimates Both Density and Score Across Distributions Without Retraining
cards.why_it_matters: As a research engineer working with generative models, score estimation, or Bayesian sampling, DiScoFormer offers a single in-context model replacing per-distribution retraining while provably generalizing KDE.
cards.summary: DiScoFormer uses a cross-attention transformer to jointly estimate probability density and score (gradient of log-density) from a finite sample in one forward pass. A shared backbone with two heads enforces a label-free consistency loss between outputs. The authors prove a single attention head subsumes KDE as a special case. Trained on Gaussian Mixture Models with closed-form targets, the model adapts to out-of-distribution inputs at inference via gradient steps on the consistency loss.

cards.title: VLK: 48K Synthetic Trajectories Enable Sim-to-Real Humanoid Loco-Manipulation via 3D Gaussian Splatting
cards.why_it_matters: For robotics-adjacent ML engineers, this pipeline demonstrates a scalable route to vision-language-kinematics supervision without human annotation, addressing a core data bottleneck in embodied agents.
cards.summary: VLK addresses the lack of synchronized egocentric images, language commands, and robot-compatible kinematic data by generating 48,000 paired trajectories synthetically. 3D Gaussian Splatting reconstructs metric-scale indoor scenes; navigation and object-interaction trajectories are synthesized using privileged scene information; egocentric observations are rendered post-hoc. A VLK policy predicts short-horizon whole-body kinematics, converted to actions via a whole-body tracker, and evaluated on a physical Unitree G1.

cards.title: WorldEvolver: Self-Evolving World Models Improve LLM Agent Planning via Test-Time Memory Revision
cards.why_it_matters: Directly relevant to researchers building LLM agents: WorldEvolver shows test-time context revision—without touching model weights—can meaningfully improve both world model accuracy and downstream planning success.
cards.summary: WorldEvolver revises a world model's deployment-time context while keeping the downstream agent and all parameters frozen. Three modules work together: Episodic Memory (retrieval-based simulation of real transitions), Semantic Memory (heuristic rules extracted from prediction-observation mismatches), and Selective Foresight (filtering low-confidence predictions before injecting them into agent context). Evaluated on ALFWorld and ScienceWorld, WorldEvolver achieves the highest prediction accuracy across three backbones and leads other world model baselines on downstream agent success rate.

cards.title: Muon Optimizer Enables Stable Asynchronous Pipeline-Parallel LLM Pretraining Up to 10B Parameters
cards.why_it_matters: Any engineer running large-scale LLM pretraining should know that optimizer choice—not asynchrony itself—determines whether pipeline bubbles can be eliminated without accuracy loss.
cards.summary: PipeDream-2BW's constant one-step gradient delay was believed to cause instability, limiting adoption of asynchronous pipeline parallelism. This paper shows the degradation is optimizer-dependent: AdamW suffers severe degradation under one-step delay, while Muon is robustly stable. An optimizer-agnostic Error Feedback-inspired correction further mitigates delay effects, with convergence proofs provided for Muon. Experiments on models up to 10B parameters confirm the approach matches synchronous training performance.

cards.title: LeVo 2: Hybrid LLM-Diffusion Framework Approaches Commercial Systems on Full-Length Song Generation
cards.why_it_matters: The hierarchical modeling and staged post-training strategy (SFT → offline DPO → semi-online DPO) is directly applicable to any researcher working on controllable generation or preference alignment pipelines.
cards.summary: LeVo 2 combines a language model (LeLM) with a diffusion-based Music Codec for full-length song generation. LeLM first predicts mixed tokens for semantic planning, then predicts vocal and accompaniment tokens in parallel. An aesthetics-guided pre-training assigns musicality-tier conditions to large-scale data; progressive post-training applies SFT, large-scale offline DPO, and closed-loop semi-online DPO sequentially. Expert listening tests show LeVo 2 outperforms open-source baselines on six subjective dimensions and approaches leading commercial systems on several metrics.

cards.title: Survey: Authentication Protocols for Quantum Networks—Candidates, Trade-offs, and QKD Integration
cards.why_it_matters: For researchers building or analyzing quantum ML or quantum communication systems, this review clarifies which authentication schemes are composable and scalable, a prerequisite often glossed over in protocol papers.
cards.summary: This review distinguishes three authentication flavors—classical message authentication, quantum message authentication, and entity authentication—comparing representative protocols on security assumptions, composability, and scalability. Hardware-assisted approaches are also covered. A detailed QKD case study anchors the discussion, with extensions to protocols beyond QKD where authentication roles are more complex. The authors argue authentication is not an intrinsic bottleneck but must be matched explicitly to functionality and deployment assumptions.

cards.title: First Proof of Random-Matrix Spectral Ramp in a Static, Geometrically Local Many-Body Hamiltonian
cards.why_it_matters: A landmark theoretical result in quantum chaos: proving the spectral form factor ramp in a time-independent local Hamiltonian closes a long-standing gap left by the Bertini-Kos-Prosen result restricted to Floquet systems.
cards.summary: Building on the Bertini-Kos-Prosen (BKP) result for dual-unitary Floquet circuits and a Feynman-Kitaev clock construction, the authors embed the Floquet quasienergy spectrum of a dual-unitary circuit into the energy spectrum of a static local Hamiltonian. They prove the static Hamiltonian's connected spectral form factor inherits the BKP linear-growth ramp within a symmetry sector. This is claimed as the first proof of a spectral ramp in a time-independent, geometrically local many-body system with finite local Hilbert space dimension.
